questions.question: Explain the procedure for converting a context‑free grammar G into an equivalent pushdown automaton M that accepts L(G) by empty stack. Give the formal transition function δ.
keyPoints: Use a start state and an accept state; push the start symbol onto the stack.
For each production A → α, add a transition (q, ε, A) → (q, α).
For each terminal a in the input, add a transition (q, a, ε) → (q, ε) that consumes a.
Transition function δ is defined as: δ(q, ε, S) = {(q, ε)} and δ(q, a, ε) = {(q, ε)}.

questions.question: Given the CFG G: S → aSb | ε, construct a PDA that accepts the language by final state. Show the transition diagram or transition function.
keyPoints: Start in q0, push S onto stack.
For S → aSb, transition on input a (or ε) that pushes a S b onto stack.
For S → ε, use ε‑transition to pop S.
Add a final state qf reachable when the stack becomes empty after consuming the whole input.

questions.question: Prove formally that every context‑free language can be recognized by a pushdown automaton. In your proof, start from a CFG G in Chomsky Normal Form and describe the construction of a PDA that simulates leftmost derivations, illustrating how the PDA accepts exactly the strings generated by G.
keyPoints: Convert G to CNF; introduce new start variable S0 and eliminate ε‑productions, unit productions, and useless symbols.
Construct PDA M = (Q, Σ, Γ, δ, q0, Z0, F) where Q = {q, q_accept}, Γ = (V ∪ T) ∪ {Z0}.
For each production A → BC add transition (q, ε, A) → (q, BC); for A → a add (q, a, A) → (q, ε).
Show that M accepts a string w iff there exists a leftmost derivation S ⇒*_lm w in G, using induction on the length of the derivation.

questions.question: Given a pushdown automaton M that accepts by empty stack, construct an equivalent context‑free grammar G such that L(G) = N(M). Explain the construction of productions in G from each transition of M, and prove that the language generated by G equals the language accepted by M.
keyPoints: Create a start variable S; for each state p in M introduce a variable [pXq] representing that the PDA can start in p with X on stack and finish in q with empty stack.
For each transition (p, a, X) → (q, ε) add production [pXq] → a; for (p, a, X) → (q, Y1Y2…Yk) add productions [pXq] → a [qY1r1] [r1Y2r2] … [rk-1Ykq] for all intermediate states r_i.
Prove by induction on the length of the computation that [pXq] ⇒* w iff M can read w, pop X and go from p to q.
Conclude L(G) = N(M), thus CFGs and PDAs are equivalent in expressive power.
